vision_report.temporal_deltas: Across the 5-day sequence, there is zero evidence of plant growth or presence. The pots appear to have been empty or cleared of all biological matter throughout the entire observation window.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-functional. There are no living specimens to evaluate. The environment is strictly abiotic.
vision_report.anomalies: The primary anomaly is the total absence of the registered plants. The presence of a spoon in the yellow pot suggests human intervention or a non-botanical use of the container.
vision_report.narrative_description: A meticulous audit of the provided image sequence reveals that the desk-based biome is currently devoid of all registered plant life. While the containers (P1-P4) and the scale anchor (white rabbit) are present, the biological components are entirely missing. The soil appears undisturbed, suggesting either a complete removal of specimens prior to the start of the sequence or a failure of the plants to establish/survive. No growth, decay, or structural changes were observed over the 5-day period.
